NKJV.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, To the saints who are in Ephesus, and faithful in Christ Jesus:
NKJV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ,
NKJV.4: just as He chose us in Him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before Him in love,
NKJV.5: having predestined us to adoption as sons by Jesus Christ to Himself, according to the good pleasure of His will,
NKJV.6: to the praise of the glory of His grace, by which He made us accepted in the Beloved.
NKJV.7: In Him we have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of His grace
NKJV.8: which He made to abound toward us in all wisdom and prudence,
NKJV.9: having made known to us the mystery of His will, according to His good pleasure which He purposed in Himself,
NKJV.10: that in the dispensation of the fullness of the times He might gather together in one all things in Christ, both which are in heaven and which are on earth—in Him.
NKJV.11: In Him also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestined according to the purpose of Him who works all things according to the counsel of His will,
NKJV.12: that we who first trusted in Christ should be to the praise of His glory.
NKJV.13: In Him you also trusted, after you heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation; in whom also, having believed, you were sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise,
NKJV.14: who is the guarantee of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, to the praise of His glory.
NKJV.15: Therefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
NKJV.16: do not cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers:
NKJV.17: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Him,
NKJV.18: the eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that you may know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
NKJV.19: and what is the exceeding greatness of His power toward us who believe, according to the working of His mighty power
NKJV.20: which He worked in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places,
NKJV.21: far above all principality and power and might and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in that which is to come.
NKJV.22: And He put all things under His feet, and gave Him to be head over all things to the church,
NKJV.23: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.

ESV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, To the saints who are in Ephesus, and are faithful  in Christ Jesus:
ESV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places,
ESV.4: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before him. In love
ESV.5: he predestined us  for adoption to himself as sons through Jesus Christ, according to the purpose of his will,
ESV.6: to the praise of his glorious grace, with which he has blessed us in the Beloved.
ESV.7: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace,
ESV.8: which he lavished upon us, in all wisdom and insight
ESV.9: making known  to us the mystery of his will, according to his purpose, which he set forth in Christ
ESV.10: as a plan for the fullness of time, to unite all things in him, things in heaven and things on earth.
ESV.11: In him we have obtained an inheritance, having been predestined according to the purpose of him who works all things according to the counsel of his will,
ESV.12: so that we who were the first to hope in Christ might be to the praise of his glory.
ESV.13: In him you also, when you heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation, and believed in him, were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit,
ESV.14: who is the guarantee  of our inheritance until we acquire possession of it,  to the praise of his glory.
ESV.15: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love  toward all the saints,
ESV.16: I do not cease to give thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers,
ESV.17: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of him,
ESV.18: having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
ESV.19: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his great might
ESV.20: that he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
ESV.21: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and above every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
ESV.22: And he put all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church,
ESV.23: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.

NASB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, To the saints who are at Ephesus and [who are] faithful in Christ Jesus:
NASB.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.3: Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly [places] in Christ,
NASB.4: just as He chose us in Him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and blameless before Him. In love
NASB.5: He predestined us to adoption as sons through Jesus Christ to Himself, according to the kind intention of His will,
NASB.6: to the praise of the glory of His grace, which He freely bestowed on us in the Beloved.
NASB.7: In Him we have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of His grace
NASB.8: which He lavished on us. In all wisdom and insight
NASB.9: He made known to us the mystery of His will, according to His kind intention which He purposed in Him
NASB.10: with a view to an administration suitable to the fullness of the times, [that is], the summing up of all things in Christ, things in the heavens and things on the earth. In Him
NASB.11: also we have obtained an inheritance, having been predestined according to His purpose who works all things after the counsel of His will,
NASB.12: to the end that we who were the first to hope in Christ would be to the praise of His glory.
NASB.13: In Him, you also, after listening to the message of truth, the gospel of your salvation-- having also believed, you were sealed in Him with the Holy Spirit of promise,
NASB.14: who is given as a pledge of our inheritance, with a view to the redemption of [God's own] possession, to the praise of His glory.
NASB.15: For this reason I too, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which [exists] among you and your love for all the saints,
NASB.16: do not cease giving thanks for you, while making mention [of you] in my prayers;
NASB.17: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of Him.
NASB.18: [I pray that] the eyes of your heart may be enlightened, so that you will know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
NASB.19: and what is the surpassing greatness of His power toward us who believe. [These are] in accordance with the working of the strength of His might
NASB.20: which He brought about in Christ, when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly [places],
NASB.21: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
NASB.22: And He put all things in subjection under His feet, and gave Him as head over all things to the church,
NASB.23: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, chosen by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. I am writing to God’s holy people in Ephesus, who are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
NLT.2: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.3: All praise to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly realms because we are united with Christ.
NLT.4: Even before he made the world, God loved us and chose us in Christ to be holy and without fault in his eyes.
NLT.5: God decided in advance to adopt us into his own family by bringing us to himself through Jesus Christ. This is what he wanted to do, and it gave him great pleasure.
NLT.6: So we praise God for the glorious grace he has poured out on us who belong to his dear Son.
NLT.7: He is so rich in kindness and grace that he purchased our freedom with the blood of his Son and forgave our sins.
NLT.8: He has showered his kindness on us, along with all wisdom and understanding.
NLT.9: God has now revealed to us his mysterious will regarding Christ — which is to fulfill his own good plan.
NLT.10: And this is the plan: At the right time he will bring everything together under the authority of Christ — everything in heaven and on earth.
NLT.11: Furthermore, because we are united with Christ, we have received an inheritance from God, for he chose us in advance, and he makes everything work out according to his plan.
NLT.12: God’s purpose was that we Jews who were the first to trust in Christ would bring praise and glory to God.
NLT.13: And now you Gentiles have also heard the truth, the Good News that God saves you. And when you believed in Christ, he identified you as his own by giving you the Holy Spirit, whom he promised long ago.
NLT.14: The Spirit is God’s guarantee that he will give us the inheritance he promised and that he has purchased us to be his own people. He did this so we would praise and glorify him.
NLT.15: Ever since I first heard of your strong faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for God’s people everywhere,
NLT.16: I have not stopped thanking God for you. I pray for you constantly,
NLT.17: asking God, the glorious Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, to give you spiritual wisdom and insight so that you might grow in your knowledge of God.
NLT.18: I pray that your hearts will be flooded with light so that you can understand the confident hope he has given to those he called — his holy people who are his rich and glorious inheritance.
NLT.19: I also pray that you will understand the incredible greatness of God’s power for us who believe him. This is the same mighty power
NLT.20: that raised Christ from the dead and seated him in the place of honor at God’s right hand in the heavenly realms.
NLT.21: Now he is far above any ruler or authority or power or leader or anything else — not only in this world but also in the world to come.
NLT.22: God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of the church.
NLT.23: And the church is his body; it is made full and complete by Christ, who fills all things everywhere with himself.

WEB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, to the saints who are at Ephesus, and the faithful in Christ Jesus:
WEB.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ;
WEB.4: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and without defect before him in love;
WEB.5: having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire,
WEB.6: to the praise of the glory of his grace, by which he freely gave us favor in the Beloved,
WEB.7: in whom we have our redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace,
WEB.8: which he made to abound toward us in all wisdom and prudence,
WEB.9: making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he purposed in him
WEB.10: to an administration of the fullness of the times, to sum up all things in Christ, the things in the heavens, and the things on the earth, in him;
WEB.11: in whom also we were assigned an inheritance, having been foreordained according to the purpose of him who does all things after the counsel of his will;
WEB.12: to the end that we should be to the praise of his glory, we who had before hoped in Christ:
WEB.13: in whom you also, having heard the word of the truth, the Good News of your salvation — in whom, having also believed, you were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit,
WEB.14: who is a pledge of our inheritance, to the redemption of God’s own possession, to the praise of his glory.
WEB.15: For this cause I also, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which is among you, and the love which you have toward all the saints,
WEB.16: don’t cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers,
WEB.17: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him;
WEB.18: having the eyes of your hearts  enlightened, that you may know what is the hope of his calling, and what are the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
WEB.19: and what is the exceeding greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to that working of the strength of his might
WEB.20: which he worked in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and made him to sit at his right hand in the heavenly places,
WEB.21: far above all rule, and authority, and power, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age, but also in that which is to come.
WEB.22: He put all things in subjection under his feet, and gave him to be head over all things for the assembly,
WEB.23: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓛ</sup> an apostle of Christ Jesus by God’s will: <sup>ⓜ</sup> To the faithful saints <sup>ⓝ</sup> in Christ Jesus <sup>[1]</sup> at Ephesus. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.3: Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavens in Christ. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.4: For he chose us in him, before the foundation of the world, to be holy and blameless in love before him. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.5: He predestined us to be adopted as sons through Jesus Christ for himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
CSB17.6: to the praise of his glorious grace <sup>ⓓ</sup> that he lavished on us in the Beloved One. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.7: In him we have redemption <sup>ⓕ</sup> through his blood, the forgiveness <sup>ⓖ</sup> of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace
CSB17.8: that he richly poured out on us with all wisdom and understanding. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.9: He made known to us the mystery of his will, <sup>ⓗ</sup> according to his good pleasure that he purposed in Christ <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.10: as a plan for the right time <sup>[5]</sup>  — to bring everything together in Christ, <sup>ⓙ</sup> both things in heaven and things on earth <sup>ⓚ</sup> in him. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.11: In him we have also received an inheritance, <sup>[6]</sup> because we were predestined <sup>ⓜ</sup> according to the plan of the one who works out everything in agreement with the purpose of his will,
CSB17.12: so that we who had already put our hope <sup>ⓝ</sup> in Christ might bring praise to his glory. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: In him you also were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit <sup>ⓑ</sup> when you heard the word of truth, <sup>ⓒ</sup> the gospel of your salvation, and when you believed.
CSB17.14: The Holy Spirit is the down payment <sup>ⓓ</sup> of our inheritance, until the redemption of the possession, to the praise of his glory.
CSB17.15: This is why, since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
CSB17.16: I never stop giving thanks for you as I remember you in my prayers.
CSB17.17: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, <sup>[7]</sup> would give you the Spirit <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> of wisdom and revelation <sup>ⓕ</sup> in the knowledge of him.
CSB17.18: I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened so that you may know what is the hope <sup>ⓖ</sup> of his calling, <sup>ⓗ</sup> what is the wealth of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
CSB17.19: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power <sup>ⓘ</sup> toward us who believe, according to the mighty working of his strength.
CSB17.20: He exercised this power in Christ by raising him from the dead and seating him at his right hand <sup>ⓙ</sup> in the heavens <sup>ⓚ</sup>  —
CSB17.21: far above every ruler and authority, power and dominion, and every title given, <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup> not only in this age but also in the one to come. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.22: And he subjected everything under his feet [10] ⓝ and appointed him [11] as head ⓐ over everything for the church,
CSB17.23: which is his body, <sup>ⓑ</sup> the fullness <sup>ⓒ</sup> of the one who fills all things <sup>ⓓ</sup> in every way.

AMP.1:
Paul, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Christ Jesus (the Messiah, the Anointed), by the will of God [that is, by His purpose and choice],
To the saints (God's people) who are at Ephesus and are faithful and loyal and steadfast in Christ Jesus:
AMP.2: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.3: Blessed and worthy of praise be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly realms in Christ,
AMP.4: just as [in His love] He chose us in Christ [actually selected us for Himself as His own] before the foundation of the world, so that we would be holy [that is, consecrated, set apart for Him, purpose-driven] and blameless in His sight. In love
AMP.5: He predestined and lovingly planned for us to be adopted to Himself as [His own] children through Jesus Christ, in accordance with the kind intention and good pleasure of His will—
AMP.6: to the praise of His glorious grace and favor, which He so freely bestowed on us in the Beloved [His Son, Jesus Christ].
AMP.7: In Him we have redemption [that is, our deliverance and salvation] through His blood, [which paid the penalty for our sin and resulted in] the forgiveness and complete pardon of our sin, in accordance with the riches of His grace
AMP.8: which He lavished on us. In all wisdom and understanding [with practical insight]
AMP.9: He made known to us the mystery of His will according to His good pleasure, which He purposed in Christ,
AMP.10: with regard to the fulfillment of the times [that is, the end of history, the climax of the ages]—to bring all things together in Christ, [both] things in the heavens and things on the earth.
AMP.11: In Him also we have received an inheritance [a destiny—we were claimed by God as His own], having been predestined (chosen, appointed beforehand) according to the purpose of Him who works everything in agreement with the counsel and design of His will,
AMP.12: so that we who were the first to hope in Christ [who first put our confidence in Him as our Lord and Savior] would exist to the praise of His glory.
AMP.13: In Him, you also, when you heard the word of truth, the good news of your salvation, and [as a result] believed in Him, were stamped with the seal of the promised Holy Spirit [the One promised by Christ] as owned and protected [by God].
AMP.14: The Spirit is the guarantee [the first installment, the pledge, a foretaste] of our inheritance until the redemption of God's own [purchased] possession [His believers], to the praise of His glory.
AMP.15: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people,
AMP.16: I do not cease to give thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers;
AMP.17: [I always pray] that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may grant you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation [that gives you a deep and personal and intimate insight] into the true knowledge of Him [for we know the Father through the Son].
AMP.18: And [I pray] that the eyes of your heart [the very center and core of your being] may be enlightened [flooded with light by the Holy Spirit], so that you will know and cherish the hope [the divine guarantee, the confident expectation] to which He has called you, the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints (God's people),
AMP.19: and [so that you will begin to know] what the immeasurable and unlimited and surpassing greatness of His [active, spiritual] power is in us who believe. These are in accordance with the working of His mighty strength
AMP.20: which He produced in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His own right hand in the heavenly places,
AMP.21: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion [whether angelic or human], and [far above] every name that is named [above every title that can be conferred], not only in this age and world but also in the one to come.
AMP.22: And He put all things [in every realm] in subjection under Christ's feet, and appointed Him as [supreme and authoritative] head over all things in the church,
AMP.23: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills and completes all things in all [believers].

MSG.1: I, Paul, am under God's plan as an apostle, a special agent of Christ Jesus, writing to you faithful Christians in Ephesus.
MSG.2: I greet you with the grace and peace poured into our lives by God our Father and our Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.3: How blessed is God! And what a blessing he is! He's the Father of our Master, Jesus Christ, and takes us to the high places of blessing in him.
MSG.4: Long before he laid down earth's foundations, he had us in mind, had settled on us as the focus of his love, to be made whole and holy by his love.
MSG.5: Long, long ago he decided to adopt us into his family through Jesus Christ. (What pleasure he took in planning this!)
MSG.6: He wanted us to enter into the celebration of his lavish gift-giving by the hand of his beloved Son.
MSG.7: Because of the sacrifice of the Messiah, his blood poured out on the altar of the Cross, we're a free people—free of penalties and punishments chalked up by all our misdeeds. And not just barely free, either. Abundantly free!
MSG.8: He thought of everything, provided for everything we could possibly need,
MSG.9: letting us in on the plans he took such delight in making. He set it all out before us in Christ,
MSG.10: a long-range plan in which everything would be brought together and summed up in him, everything in deepest heaven, everything on planet earth.
MSG.11: It's in Christ that we find out who we are and what we are living for. Long before we first heard of Christ and got our hopes up, he had his eye on us, had designs on us for glorious living,
MSG.12: part of the overall purpose he is working out in everything and everyone.
MSG.13: It's in Christ that you, once you heard the truth and believed it (this Message of your salvation), found yourselves home free—signed, sealed, and delivered by the Holy Spirit.
MSG.14: This signet from God is the first installment on what's coming, a reminder that we'll get everything God has planned for us, a praising and glorious life.
MSG.15: That's why, when I heard of the solid trust you have in the Master Jesus and your outpouring of love to all the Christians,
MSG.16: I couldn't stop thanking God for you—every time I prayed, I'd think of you and give thanks.
MSG.17: But I do more than thank. I ask—ask the God of our Master, Jesus Christ, the God of glory—to make you intelligent and discerning in knowing him personally,
MSG.18: your eyes focused and clear, so that you can see exactly what it is he is calling you to do, grasp the immensity of this glorious way of life he has for Christians,
MSG.19: oh, the utter extravagance of his work in us who trust him—endless energy, boundless strength!
MSG.20: All this energy issues from Christ: God raised him from death and set him on a throne in deep heaven,
MSG.21: in charge of running the universe, everything from galaxies to governments, no name and no power exempt from his rule. And not just for the time being, but forever.
MSG.22: He is in charge of it all, has the final word on everything. At the center of all this, Christ rules the church.
MSG.23: The church, you see, is not peripheral to the world; the world is peripheral to the church. The church is Christ's body, in which he speaks and acts, by which he fills everything with his presence.

NIV.1:
Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God,
To the saints in Ephesus, the faithful in Christ Jesus:
NIV.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.3:
Spiritual Blessings in Christ
Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in the heavenly realms with every spiritual blessing in Christ.
NIV.4: For he chose us in him before the creation of the world to be holy and blameless in his sight. In love
NIV.5: he predestined us to be adopted as his sons through Jesus Christ, in accordance with his pleasure and will—
NIV.6: to the praise of his glorious grace, which he has freely given us in the One he loves.
NIV.7: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God’s grace
NIV.8: that he lavished on us with all wisdom and understanding.
NIV.9: And he made known to us the mystery of his will according to his good pleasure, which he purposed in Christ,
NIV.10: to be put into effect when the times will have reached their fulfillment—to bring all things in heaven and on earth together under one head, even Christ.
NIV.11: In him we were also chosen, having been predestined according to the plan of him who works out everything in conformity with the purpose of his will,
NIV.12: in order that we, who were the first to hope in Christ, might be for the praise of his glory.
NIV.13: And you also were included in Christ when you heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation. Having believed, you were marked in him with a seal, the promised Holy Spirit,
NIV.14: who is a deposit guaranteeing our inheritance until the redemption of those who are God’s possession—to the praise of his glory.
NIV.15:
Thanksgiving and Prayer
For this reason, ever since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
NIV.16: I have not stopped giving thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers.
NIV.17: I keep asking that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and revelation, so that you may know him better.
NIV.18: I pray also that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened in order that you may know the hope to which he has called you, the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
NIV.19: and his incomparably great power for us who believe. That power is like the working of his mighty strength,
NIV.20: which he exerted in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly realms,
NIV.21: far above all rule and authority, power and dominion, and every title that can be given, not only in the present age but also in the one to come.
NIV.22: And God placed all things under his feet and appointed him to be head over everything for the church,
NIV.23: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills everything in every way.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, To the saints who are in Ephesus and are faithful in Christ Jesus:
NRSVCE.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places,
NRSVCE.4: just as he chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world to be holy and blameless before him in love.
NRSVCE.5: He destined us for adoption as his children through Jesus Christ, according to the good pleasure of his will,
NRSVCE.6: to the praise of his glorious grace that he freely bestowed on us in the Beloved.
NRSVCE.7: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace
NRSVCE.8: that he lavished on us. With all wisdom and insight
NRSVCE.9: he has made known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure that he set forth in Christ,
NRSVCE.10: as a plan for the fullness of time, to gather up all things in him, things in heaven and things on earth.
NRSVCE.11: In Christ we have also obtained an inheritance, having been destined according to the purpose of him who accomplishes all things according to his counsel and will,
NRSVCE.12: so that we, who were the first to set our hope on Christ, might live for the praise of his glory.
NRSVCE.13: In him you also, when you had heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation, and had believed in him, were marked with the seal of the promised Holy Spirit;
NRSVCE.14: this is the pledge of our inheritance toward redemption as God’s own people, to the praise of his glory.
NRSVCE.15: I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love toward all the saints, and for this reason
NRSVCE.16: I do not cease to give thanks for you as I remember you in my prayers.
NRSVCE.17: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation as you come to know him,
NRSVCE.18: so that, with the eyes of your heart enlightened, you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance among the saints,
NRSVCE.19: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power for us who believe, according to the working of his great power.
NRSVCE.20: God put this power to work in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
NRSVCE.21: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and above every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the age to come.
NRSVCE.22: And he has put all things under his feet and has made him the head over all things for the church,
NRSVCE.23: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.

NET.1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, to the saints [in Ephesus], the faithful in Christ Jesus.
NET.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.3: Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly realms in Christ.
NET.4: For he chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world that we may be holy and unblemished in his sight in love.
NET.5: He did this by predestining us to adoption as his sons through Jesus Christ, according to the pleasure of his will —
NET.6: to the praise of the glory of his grace that he has freely bestowed on us in his dearly loved Son.
NET.7: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace
NET.8: that he lavished on us in all wisdom and insight.
NET.9: He did this when he revealed to us the secret of his will, according to his good pleasure that he set forth in Christ,
NET.10: toward the administration of the fullness of the times, to head up all things in Christ — the things in heaven and the things on earth.
NET.11: In Christ we too have been claimed as God’s own possession, since we were predestined according to the one purpose of him who accomplishes all things according to the counsel of his will
NET.12: so that we, who were the first to set our hope on Christ, would be to the praise of his glory.
NET.13: And when you heard the word of truth (the gospel of your salvation) — when you believed in Christ — you were marked with the seal of the promised Holy Spirit,
NET.14: who is the down payment of our inheritance, until the redemption of God’s own possession, to the praise of his glory.
NET.15: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
NET.16: I do not cease to give thanks for you when I remember you in my prayers.
NET.17: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you spiritual wisdom and revelation in your growing knowledge of him,
NET.18: — since the eyes of your heart have been enlightened — so that you may know what is the hope of his calling, what is the wealth of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
NET.19: and what is the incomparable greatness of his power toward us who believe, as displayed in the exercise of his immense strength.
NET.20: This power he exercised in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly realms
NET.21: far above every rule and authority and power and dominion and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
NET.22: And God put all things under Christ’s feet, and he gave him to the church as head over all things.
NET.23: Now the church is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.
